text.title: Research Tool Selection Agent Workflow
text.summary: Decision-tree workflow for agent-driven tool selection, with human confirmation gate and gotchas for API-availability mismatches.

rule.statement: Use this category map as a decision tree: given phase and budget, select 1-2 tools per phase, then read phase-specific methodology files for execution details.
rule.rationale: The category map narrows tool selection; per-phase methodologies (e.g., ai-interview-analysis, synthetic-users) provide execution rules. Do not conflate navigation with execution.

rule.statement: Human confirmation required at tool selection stage; the agent recommends, the researcher confirms before any SaaS contract or pipeline build begins.
rule.rationale: Wrong tool choice is expensive to reverse mid-sprint. Tool-selection agents tend to recommend well-known tools, not the most API-accessible ones; researcher validates fit.

rule.statement: Verify API availability and rate limits independently before building a pipeline; "agent-friendly" in any reference does not guarantee automation suitability.
rule.rationale: Many SaaS tools have APIs that are poorly documented, rate-limited for automation, or require enterprise contracts to access programmatically.

rule.statement: For B2B user research with NDA constraints, verify data processing agreements before piping transcripts through any SaaS tool.
rule.rationale: Many SaaS research tools process data on third-party servers; participant confidentiality agreements may prohibit this without explicit consent.

rule.statement: Free/mid stacks cover 80% of solopreneur research needs; enterprise tools add competitive intelligence breadth, not user research depth.
rule.rationale: Enterprise tier tools (AlphaSense, Klue, Brandwatch) specialize in market and competitive intelligence. User research depth requires qualitative tools available at mid tier.

text.reference: Phase + budget → tool list filter script
Stack recommendation prompt with structured output
